title: Warm Fluffy American Corn Pancakes
description: A satvik-friendly, fermented, and fluffy American dish featuring candied peel, perfect for a brunch mood, with a wood-fired bake twist.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, cornmeal, yeast, salt, and sugar.
In a separate bowl, whisk together buttermilk, eggs, and melted butter.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
Let the batter rest in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat a wood-fired oven or a skillet on the stovetop to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Drop the batter by 1/4 cupfuls onto the preheated surface.
Cook the pancakes for 2-3 minutes, or until bubbles appear on the surface and the edges start to dry.
Flip the pancakes and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, or until golden brown.
Serve the pancakes warm, topped with your favorite toppings such as maple syrup, honey, or fresh fruit.
